app.name: vue-pure-admin
app.one_liner: An open-source, ready-to-use mid-to-back-end management system template built with Vue3, Vite, Element-Plus, TypeScript, Pinia, and Tailwind CSS using ESM.

screens.name: Error Pages
screens.purpose: Display full-screen error states (403, 404, 500) with clear messaging and recovery options.

screens.name: Main Dashboard
screens.purpose: Provides the primary navigation and workspace for managing system resources and viewing key metrics.

screens.name: System Settings
screens.purpose: Configure global application behavior including theme mode and menu layout.

rust_commands.name: list_items
rust_commands.purpose: Fetch paginated or filtered lists of system items from backend API.

rust_commands.name: load_menu_icons
rust_commands.purpose: Load SVG icons for navigation menu items from backend response.

rust_commands.name: save_item
rust_commands.purpose: Persist configuration or data changes to backend storage.

mvp_plan: week 1: Implement core layout with dynamic menu loading
week 1: Initialize project with ESM-based Vue3 + Vite scaffold
week 1: Integrate Element-Plus and Tailwind CSS
week 2: Add system settings screen with theme and layout controls
week 2: Implement SVG icon support for menu items
acceptance_tests: After enabling 'keepAlive: true' on a route, revisiting the tab restores state without reloading.
Given a user navigates to an invalid route, then they see a full-screen 404 page with a return button.
Given the backend returns an SVG string for a menu item icon, then the navigation menu renders it correctly without style issues.
When the user switches 'theme mode' in system settings, the entire UI updates consistently without flickering.
